evidence: Vrijen shared his personal interest in building AI tools as 'brain crutches' for ADHD—filling gaps between therapy, medication, and coaching.

source_display: Death by a Thousand Pilots (EdTech Trap)
evidence: Vrijen shared advice from his edtech experience: avoid the 'death by a thousand cuts' of endless unpaid pilots. Instead, seek paid pilots with scoped changes and identify design partners willing to work with the product as-is.
A cornerstone customer providing reliable revenue creates options—whether to scale, attract others, or seek investment.
He defined product-market fit using the Supreme Court's obscenity standard: 'you know it when you see it'—when you have it, you don't think about it; when you don't, you obsess over it constantly.
Special education teacher turned edtech founder, building '360' ADHD support product for ~1 year, has 65K email subscribers, moral obligation framing for B2B focus.

Both founders share similar backgrounds transitioning from non-technical fields into tech product development. V described the first year as characterized by face-plants and misplaced trust in technical partners.
V offered FOHI community referral (6,000 members, ~1,000 NYC). Both agreed edtech attracts genuinely kind people compared to other tech sectors.

The core concept is a 'marketplace of all possible futures' - allowing users to 'jump into' career simulations mapped to real jobs and companies. Users can fail safely and restart at the click of a button.
Max used the metaphor of a 'neopet' - anytime you train or deploy your AI requires 'energy,' which is where they monetize through usage-based pricing.

Maximilian emphasized that his product creates portfolios first and assessments second, acknowledging that assessments are difficult to execute effectively. He criticized HackerRank for not grounding results in actual business value.

Engineers more than one step removed from the product were most vulnerable in layoffs. Product owner and project manager roles 'are done' in the current AI-enabled landscape unless they manage P&L and have product experience.

The consensus is to aim for a sale mid-year but spend the next few months generating revenue first, which V believes will massively improve their valuation. Team reduced to 3 people, 2 founders unpaid.
Careerspan pursuing acquisition while pivoting to generate revenue through India recruiting partnership (Shivam) before targeting mid-year acquisition.
Founder of Dayoneme, launching career simulation product in ~1 month. Anti-assessment stance, portfolio-first philosophy, shared worldview on systemic obsolescence in talent markets.

V introduced context engineering as the foundational practice: not prompting the AI directly, but cultivating the context it uses. Three levels: Conversation (tactical), Environment (persistent context), Pipeline (external data integration).
V argued the core issue isn't a technology or tactics problem but a mental model problem—users collect techniques without understanding how they integrate into effective systems.
Ask the AI to create an evaluation rubric, fill it out, assign scores, and iterate output until achieving the highest level.
Expansive generation (expand option set) vs. critical tightening (attack, break down, shore up).

Logan demonstrated analysis of 18 years of LinkedIn data (48 CSV files, 5,000+ messages). Discovered 23 high-value DMs accidentally ghosted, 1,800% increase in connections from posting, network mismatch vs imported contacts.

source_display: Job Security Dead, Career Security Needed
evidence: Logan's conviction: 'job security is dead and you need to be building career security for yourself... it has a lot to do with being findable and being visible'
Ben: 'you can unhobble your AI a lot when you give it access to your files and data... when you give the AI its own home' — Anthropic team reference.

source_display: Plan Files as Future of Coding
evidence: Plan files that mix prose and code in stepwise fashion. Positioned as the future of coding: planning systems through narrative structure rather than pure code alone.
Co-founder of Zo Computer. V described as 'power Zo user.' Demonstrated SMS-based coding workflows, Spotify data analysis, passive-moment productivity.
Careerspan co-founder. Demonstrated LinkedIn data analysis (18 years, 48 CSVs). Metacognitive patterns: narrative before data, never accept first answer, ask 'delight me'.
